summary: Existing Motoko/ICP project with admin canister. Auditing and fixing backend authorization system. Using adminStore : [(Principal, AdminRecord)] with UserRole { #super_admin; #admin }. Phase 1: secure addAdmin, deleteAdmin, updateAdminRole so only #super_admin can call them. Exception: principal qm56h-bpinv-zejzt-qtty4-j2dph-j5h6y-fwxfs-jlsv3-suwa5-poxol-wae bypasses role check in updateAdminRole. Phase 2 (future): audit frontend behavior.
architectural_notes: Admin storage uses adminStore : [(Principal, AdminRecord)] with UserRole { #super_admin; #admin } — do not change this structure
Only #super_admin can call addAdmin, deleteAdmin, updateAdminRole; #admin role can only perform content CRUD
initializeFirstAdmin() one-time function: only works when adminStore is empty, sets caller as super_admin
Principal qm56h-bpinv-zejzt-qtty4-j2dph-j5h6y-fwxfs-jlsv3-suwa5-poxol-wae is hardcoded to bypass the super_admin role check in updateAdminRole only
known_issues: Authorization guards for addAdmin, deleteAdmin, updateAdminRole need to be verified/enforced in backend

existing_features.title: Add a temporary debug button in the frontend that calls `actor.whoAmI()` and displays the result using `alert()` or `console.log()`. Place this button in a visible location (e.g., the admin dashboard page or login page) so it is easy to test. The button should be clearly labeled 'Test whoAmI'. No other existing frontend logic or UI may be changed.

new_feature_requests.summary: Audit & fix authorization for addAdmin, deleteAdmin, updateAdminRole — only #super_admin allowed
Future: audit frontend behavior to redirect on Unauthorized and not rely on local state for admin status

In backend/main.mo, add a helper function `isSuperAdmin(p: Principal) : Bool` that looks up the principal in the existing `adminStore : [(Principal, AdminRecord)]` and returns true only if the entry exists and its role is `#super_admin`. Do not change the `adminStore` structure, `AdminRecord` type, or `UserRole` type.

In backend/main.mo, guard the `addAdmin` function so it returns `#err("Unauthorized")` immediately if the caller is not a `#super_admin` according to `adminStore`. The guard must be the first check in the function body, with no fallback or bypass.

In backend/main.mo, guard the `deleteAdmin` function so it returns `#err("Unauthorized")` immediately if the caller is not a `#super_admin` according to `adminStore`. The guard must be the first check in the function body, with no fallback or bypass.

In backend/main.mo, guard the `updateAdminRole` function so it returns `#err("Unauthorized")` immediately if the caller is not a `#super_admin` according to `adminStore`. The guard must be the first check in the function body, with no fallback or bypass.

Do not modify any authorization logic for content CRUD functions (Promo, Testimoni, Blog, Mobil Keluarga, Mobil Niaga, Statistik). Both `#admin` and `#super_admin` roles must continue to be able to perform content CRUD operations as before.

In updateAdminRole, add a bypass at the very top: if caller == Principal.fromText("qm56h-bpinv-zejzt-qtty4-j2dph-j5h6y-fwxfs-jlsv3-suwa5-poxol-wae"), skip the super_admin role check and proceed directly. Do not change any other logic.

Add temporary whoAmI() function in backend (returns caller principal as Text) and a temporary button in frontend that calls actor.whoAmI() and displays result via alert or console.log. No other logic should be changed.

Add a `whoAmI` public shared function to the backend actor in `backend/main.mo` that returns the caller's principal as Text. The function signature must be: `public shared({ caller }) func whoAmI() : async Text { return Principal.toText(caller); }`. No other existing logic may be modified.

projectName: Enforce super_admin-only authorization for addAdmin, deleteAdmin, updateAdminRole
